verses.text: If his oblation be a peace offering of the herd — male or female, without blemish. Lay his hand on its head, kill at the door; priests sprinkle blood round the altar. Offer the fat covering the inwards, the kidneys with fat, the caul above the liver. Priests burn it — sweet savor to the LORD. Guidance: Peace = shalom offering. Male or female, both received. The fat — the richest part — goes to God; the worshipper takes the rest home to feast. Reconciliation ends at a table.

If his oblation be a peace offering of the herd — male or female, without blemish. Lay his hand on its head, kill at the door; priests sprinkle blood round the altar. Offer the fat covering the inwards, the kidneys with fat, the caul above the liver. Priests burn it — sweet savor to the LORD. Guidance: Peace = shalom offering. Male or female, both received. The fat — the richest part — goes to God; the worshipper takes the rest home to feast. Reconciliation ends at a table.

If of the flock — male or female. If a lamb, bring before the LORD, lay his hand, kill at the tabernacle; priests sprinkle blood. Offer the fat — whole rump by the backbone, fat on the inwards, kidneys, caul above the liver. Burn it — food of the fire offering to the LORD. Guidance: A whole tail of fat goes up. Even from a single lamb, the choice cut is God's. He calls this His food — sharing the meal with His people on terms only He sets.

If of the flock — male or female. If a lamb, bring before the LORD, lay his hand, kill at the tabernacle; priests sprinkle blood. Offer the fat — whole rump by the backbone, fat on the inwards, kidneys, caul above the liver. Burn it — food of the fire offering to the LORD. Guidance: A whole tail of fat goes up. Even from a single lamb, the choice cut is God's. He calls this His food — sharing the meal with His people on terms only He sets.

If a goat — bring before the LORD, lay his hand, kill at the tabernacle; priests sprinkle blood. Offer the fat covering the inwards, kidneys, caul above the liver. The priest burns it — all the fat is the LORD's. A perpetual statute: eat neither fat nor blood. Guidance: A line drawn forever: no fat, no blood. The fat is God's portion; the blood is the life God gave. Both are His. Israel learns to keep their hands off what belongs to heaven.
